cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out why Ms. Leibfried asked the author to recite a passage in front of the class. So the question is multiple choice with options A to D. First, I'll go back to the passage to gather clues. The author mentions that Ms. Leibfried noticed her interest in reading and writing and made her feel less shy. She even suggested a favorite novel. The big project was a book report, but Ms. Leibfried suggested the author do something a little different. The author chose a passage from "The Bell Jar" to recite. After the recitation, the class applauded, and the teacher praised her for choosing a sensitive piece and delivering it beautifully. This made the author feel confident for the first time. Now, the question is why Ms. Leibfried did this. Let's look at the options: A. To test memory. The passage isn't about memorizing; it's about reading a passage and reciting it. The teacher's comment focuses on the choice of passage and delivery, not memory. So probably not A. B. To make her earn respect. The author was shy and introverted, maybe teased. After the recital, the class applauded. The teacher's action might have been to help her gain respect, but the teacher's motivation is key. The passage says the teacher noticed her interest and made her feel less shy. So maybe B is possible, but is that the main reason? C. To deepen understanding. Reciting a passage could help understand the text, but the teacher's comment was about the choice and delivery. The author had already chosen a passage she considered best. The focus was more on presentation and confidence. Not sure if it's about understanding. D. To build relationships. The author was isolated, reading alone.
